cit.basis: Companies Income Tax Act (CITA), as amended by the Finance Acts: 0% for small companies (gross turnover ≤ ₦25m), 20% for medium companies (turnover > ₦25m and ≤ ₦100m), 30% for large companies (turnover > ₦100m). Size is turnover-based; the caller asserts classification.
cit.note: Administered by the Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS). Minimum tax, sector-specific regimes (petroleum profits tax) and pioneer-status incentives are out of scope for this SME estimator.

tertiary_education_tax.basis: Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) Act: 3% of assessable profit for medium and large companies (Finance Act 2023 rate); small companies are exempt.

reform_2025.note: Nigeria Tax Act 2025 (signed June 2025) consolidates CITA and related tax acts, effective 2026: small-company turnover threshold rises to ₦100m and the Tertiary Education Tax is replaced by a 4% Development Levy. Note only — not modelled for the 2025 ruleset.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Company-size classification is turnover-based and asserted by the caller.
Minimum tax, capital allowances, WHT, levies, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official FIRS guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
